ex.en: She runs five miles every morning.

run.en: be in charge of a business, organization, or activity and make it work

ex.en: He runs a small bakery downtown.

run.en: (of a machine or engine) work or operate

ex.en: The old engine still runs well.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 엔진은 여전히 잘 작동한다.

run.en: (of a liquid) flow in a continuous stream

ex.en: Tears ran down her cheeks.

run.en: be a candidate in an election

ex.en: She decided to run for mayor.

set.en: put something in a particular place or position

ex.en: She set the vase on the table.

set.en: establish or fix a goal, rule, or standard

ex.en: The teacher set a deadline for the essay.

set.en: a group of similar things that belong together or are used together

ex.en: He bought a set of golf clubs.

set.en: (of the sun) go down below the horizon

ex.en: The sun sets early in winter.
We have a set menu for the party.
ex.ko: 우리는 파티를 위한 고정 메뉴가 있다.

set.en: the place where a film or play is performed or filmed, built to look like a real place

ex.en: The actors gathered on set early in the morning.
ex.ko: 배우들은 이른 아침 촬영 세트에 모였다.

get.en: come to have or receive something

ex.en: I got a new phone for my birthday.

get.en: become or start to be in a particular state

ex.en: It's getting dark outside.
What time did you get home?
Sorry, I don't get the joke.
ex.ko: 미안한데, 나는 그 농담을 이해하지 못하겠어.

get.en: go and bring something or someone back

ex.en: Can you get my glasses from the kitchen?

take.en: carry or move something or someone from one place to another

ex.en: Don't forget to take an umbrella with you.
ex.ko: 우산 챙겨 가는 것 잊지 마.

take.en: reach out and hold something in your hand

ex.en: He took her hand and smiled.

take.en: travel somewhere using a particular vehicle

ex.en: I usually take the bus to work.

take.en: require a certain amount of time

ex.en: The trip takes about two hours.
ex.ko: 그 여행은 약 두 시간이 걸린다.

take.en: do an examination or attend a course

ex.en: She is going to take the driving test next week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 다음 주에 운전면허 시험을 칠 예정이다.

ex.en: Take this medicine twice a day.
ex.ko: 이 약을 하루에 두 번 복용하세요.

ex.en: She made a cake for the party.

make.en: cause someone or something to be in a particular state

ex.en: That movie made me cry.
He makes a good salary as an engineer.

make.en: decide on or reach something such as a decision or agreement

ex.en: We need to make a decision by Friday.

light.en: the natural energy that comes from the sun, fire, or a lamp, allowing you to see

ex.en: Sunlight streamed through the window.

light.en: a device such as a lamp that produces light

ex.en: Please turn off the light before you leave.
ex.ko: 나가기 전에 불 좀 꺼 주세요.

light.en: not heavy; easy to lift or carry

ex.en: This suitcase is much lighter than mine.
ex.ko: 이 여행 가방은 내 것보다 훨씬 가볍다.

light.en: (of a color) pale rather than dark

ex.en: She painted the room a light blue.
He lit a candle on the table.
ex.ko: 그는 탁자 위의 초에 불을 붙였다.

book.en: a written work printed on pages and bound together

ex.en: She read a book before going to sleep.

book.en: arrange to have a seat, room, ticket, etc. at a future time

ex.en: We booked a table for two at the restaurant.
You were right about the weather.

right.en: on or towards the side of the body that is to the east when facing north

ex.en: Turn right at the next corner.

right.en: something that you are morally or legally allowed to do or have

ex.en: Everyone has the right to education.
Stay right here until I come back.
ex.ko: 내가 돌아올 때까지 바로 여기 있어.

hold.en: have or keep something in your hand or arms

ex.en: She held the baby gently.

hold.en: organize and take part in an event

ex.en: The school holds a festival every autumn.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 매년 가을 축제를 개최한다.

hold.en: keep something for someone to use or collect later

ex.en: Can you hold my seat while I get some coffee?
ex.ko: 내가 커피 가지러 가는 동안 내 자리 좀 맡아 줄래?

hold.en: have enough space for a certain amount or number

ex.en: This hall can hold up to 500 people.
ex.ko: 이 홀은 최대 500명을 수용할 수 있다.

hold.en: wait on the phone without hanging up

ex.en: Please hold while I transfer your call.
ex.ko: 전화 연결해 드리는 동안 끊지 말고 기다려 주세요.

break.en: separate into pieces as a result of force

ex.en: He accidentally broke the window.

break.en: a short period of rest from work or activity

ex.en: Let's take a ten-minute break.

break.en: fail to keep a law, rule, or promise

ex.en: He broke his promise to call her.

break.en: do better than a previous record

ex.en: She broke the world record in swimming.
The scandal broke just before the election.

draw.en: make a picture using a pencil, pen, or crayon

ex.en: The kids drew pictures of their families.
The street performer drew a large crowd.
ex.ko: 그 거리 공연자는 많은 사람들을 끌어모았다.

draw.en: a result of a game or contest in which both sides have the same score

ex.en: The match ended in a 2-2 draw.

draw.en: reach a conclusion after considering information

ex.en: It's too early to draw any conclusions.

play.en: do things for enjoyment, especially as children do

ex.en: The children are playing in the park.
She plays the violin beautifully.

play.en: take part in a sport or game

ex.en: He plays soccer every weekend.

play.en: act the part of a character in a film or play

ex.en: She played the lead role in the movie.

play.en: a piece of writing performed by actors on stage

ex.en: We watched a play at the theater last night.

turn.en: move or cause something to move around a central point

ex.en: She turned the key and opened the door.

turn.en: change the direction you are facing or moving

ex.en: Turn left at the traffic light.

turn.en: the time when someone is allowed or expected to do something in a set order

ex.en: It's your turn to choose the movie.

turn.en: become something or reach a particular state

ex.en: The leaves turn red in autumn.

mean.en: have a particular meaning or sense

ex.en: What does this word mean?

mean.en: be serious or sincere about what you say

ex.en: I really mean it — thank you for everything.

mean.en: unkind or unpleasant to other people

ex.en: It was mean of him to laugh at her.
ex.ko: 그가 그녀를 비웃은 것은 못된 짓이었다.

kind.en: caring and helpful towards other people

ex.en: She is always kind to strangers.

kind.en: a group of things or people that share similar qualities

ex.en: What kind of music do you like?
The teacher was fair to all the students.

fair.en: an event where people buy and sell things or where rides and games are set up

ex.en: We went to the county fair last weekend.
ex.ko: 우리는 지난 주말에 지역 축제에 갔다.

fair.en: (of hair or skin) light in color

ex.en: She has fair skin and blonde hair.
ex.ko: 그녀는 흰 피부와 금발 머리를 가지고 있다.

ex.en: The forecast promises fair weather this weekend.
ex.ko: 일기예보에서 이번 주말은 맑은 날씨라고 한다.

leave.en: go away from a place or person

ex.en: She left the office at six.

leave.en: allow something to remain in a particular place or condition

ex.en: I left my umbrella on the bus.

leave.en: a period of time when someone is allowed to be away from work

ex.en: She is on maternity leave for six months.

fall.en: drop down or collapse to a lower position accidentally

ex.en: He slipped and fell on the ice.

fall.en: the season between summer and winter

ex.en: The leaves change color in the fall.

fall.en: decrease in amount, level, or value

ex.en: Prices fell sharply last month.

fall.en: come to be in a particular state, especially suddenly

ex.en: They fell in love at first sight.

miss.en: fail to catch, reach, see, or do something

ex.en: I missed the bus this morning.

miss.en: feel sad because someone is not with you

ex.en: I really miss my family.

miss.en: a title used before the name of an unmarried woman

ex.en: Miss Kim will help you with the paperwork.
ex.ko: 김 양이 서류 작업을 도와드릴 겁니다.

match.en: be similar to or go well with something else

ex.en: Her shoes match her bag perfectly.

match.en: an organized contest between two players or teams

ex.en: They watched the soccer match together.

match.en: a small stick that produces fire when you rub it against a rough surface

ex.en: He lit the candle with a match.

bank.en: an organization where people keep their money

ex.en: She works at a bank downtown.

bank.en: the land along the edge of a river

ex.en: They had a picnic on the river bank.

bark.en: (of a dog) make a short, loud sound

ex.en: The dog barked at the mailman.

bark.en: the hard outer covering of a tree

ex.en: The bark of this tree is very rough.

bat.en: a small flying animal that is active at night

ex.en: Bats sleep upside down during the day.
ex.ko: 박쥐는 낮 동안 거꾸로 매달려 잔다.

bat.en: a wooden or metal club used to hit the ball in sports like baseball

ex.en: He swung the bat and hit a home run.

bear.en: a large, heavy wild animal with thick fur

ex.en: A bear wandered into the campsite.

bear.en: accept or deal with something unpleasant, especially with patience

ex.en: She couldn't bear the pain any longer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 더 이상 그 고통을 견딜 수 없었다.

bear.en: be responsible for something, such as a cost or a duty

ex.en: The company will bear the cost of repairs.
ex.ko: 그 회사가 수리 비용을 부담할 것이다.

spring.en: the season between winter and summer

ex.en: Flowers bloom in spring.

spring.en: a coiled piece of metal that returns to its original shape after being pressed or pulled

ex.en: The mattress has springs inside it.
ex.ko: 그 매트리스 안에는 스프링이 들어 있다.

spring.en: jump or move suddenly and quickly

ex.en: The cat sprang onto the shelf.
They climbed over the rocks on the beach.

rock.en: move gently backward and forward or from side to side

ex.en: She rocked the baby to sleep.

rock.en: a type of popular music with a strong beat, usually played with electric guitars

ex.en: He loves listening to classic rock.
ex.ko: 그는 클래식 록 음악 듣는 것을 좋아한다.

ring.en: a piece of jewelry worn on a finger

ex.en: He proposed with a diamond ring.

ring.en: make a clear, loud sound like a bell

ex.en: The phone rang three times before she answered.
ex.ko: 그녀가 받기 전에 전화가 세 번 울렸다.

ring.en: an object or shape in the form of a circle

ex.en: The children stood in a ring around the tree.
ex.ko: 아이들은 나무 주위에 원을 그리며 섰다.

ring.en: the square area where boxers fight

ex.en: The boxers stepped into the ring.

watch.en: look at something for a period of time, especially something moving or changing

ex.en: We watched a movie last night.

watch.en: a small device worn on the wrist that shows the time

ex.en: He checked his watch and hurried out.
Watch your step on the stairs.

watch.en: keep someone or something under close observation

ex.en: The guard watched the entrance all night.

wave.en: a raised line of water that moves across the surface of the sea

ex.en: The children played in the waves.

wave.en: move your hand from side to side to greet someone or attract attention

ex.en: She waved at me from across the street.
ex.ko: 그녀는 길 건너편에서 나에게 손을 흔들었다.

wave.en: a sudden increase in a particular activity or feeling that spreads through a place

ex.en: The country is experiencing a heat wave.

note.en: a short piece of writing that helps you remember something

ex.en: She left a note on the fridge.

note.en: notice or pay careful attention to something

ex.en: Please note that the office is closed on Sundays.
ex.ko: 사무실은 일요일에 문을 닫는다는 점을 유의해 주세요.

note.en: a single musical sound of a particular pitch

ex.en: She hit a wrong note during the performance.
ex.ko: 그녀는 공연 중에 잘못된 음을 냈다.

ex.en: He paid with a ten-pound note.

order.en: ask for something to be brought, made, or supplied

ex.en: We ordered pizza for dinner.

order.en: the way in which things are arranged in relation to each other

ex.en: Please put the names in alphabetical order.

order.en: an instruction given by someone in authority

ex.en: The soldiers followed the general's orders.

order.en: a situation in which everything is well organized and follows rules or laws

ex.en: The police worked to restore order after the riot.
ex.ko: 경찰은 폭동 이후 질서를 회복하려 노력했다.

present.en: a thing given to someone as a gift

ex.en: She gave him a present for his birthday.

present.en: the period of time that is happening now

ex.en: Let's focus on the present instead of worrying about the future.

present.en: show or explain something to a group of people

ex.en: She presented her research at the conference.

present.en: being in a particular place at a particular time

ex.en: All the students were present for the exam.

close.en: shut something so there is no opening

ex.en: Please close the door when you leave.
ex.ko: 나갈 때 문 좀 닫아 주세요.

ex.en: The hotel is close to the beach.

close.en: having a strong friendship or bond with someone

ex.en: She is very close to her sister.

park.en: a public area with grass and trees where people can walk and relax

ex.en: They had a picnic in the park.

park.en: leave a vehicle somewhere for a period of time

ex.en: He parked the car in front of the building.
There was a cool breeze in the evening.
That's a really cool jacket.
Let the soup cool before you eat it.

cool.en: calm and not showing much emotion, especially under pressure

ex.en: She stayed cool during the emergency.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 비상 상황에서도 침착함을 유지했다.

ex.en: The towels are already dry.

dry.en: remove moisture from something, or become free of moisture

ex.en: Hang the clothes outside to dry.
The lecture was long and dry.

hard.en: firm and solid; not easy to press or break

ex.en: The rock felt hard and cold.
This math problem is really hard.

hard.en: with a lot of effort or energy

ex.en: She worked hard to pass the exam.

sharp.en: having a fine cutting edge or point

ex.en: Be careful, the knife is very sharp.

sharp.en: quick to notice, understand, or judge things

ex.en: She has a sharp mind for numbers.
ex.ko: 그녀는 숫자에 예리한 두뇌를 가지고 있다.

ex.en: He felt a sharp pain in his chest.
There was a sharp rise in prices.
The meeting starts at nine sharp.

charge.en: ask someone to pay a particular amount of money for something

ex.en: The hotel charges extra for breakfast.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 조식에 추가 요금을 청구한다.

charge.en: an amount of money that you must pay for goods or services

ex.en: There is no charge for delivery.

charge.en: fill a battery with electrical power

ex.en: I need to charge my phone.

charge.en: the responsibility for taking care of or controlling something

ex.en: She is in charge of the marketing team.

charge.en: formally accuse someone of a crime

ex.en: He was charged with theft.
What is your current job?

current.en: a flow of electricity through a wire or circuit

ex.en: An electric current flows through the wire.

current.en: a continuous flowing movement of water or air in one direction

ex.en: The boat was pulled by a strong current.
